activities_projects.title: Farming System Evaluation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze advantages and limitations of different farming systems
steps: Study primitive subsistence, intensive subsistence, and commercial farming
List the advantages and disadvantages of each system
Suggest which system is most sustainable for India's future agriculture and justify your answer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking about agricultural practices
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of reasoning and justification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Climate and Crop Suitability Study
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how climate influences crop production
steps: Select three crops such as rice, wheat and cotton
Analyze the temperature, rainfall and soil conditions required for each crop
Explain why these crops are concentrated in specific regions of India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the relationship between climate and agriculture

activities_projects.title: Cropping Pattern Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Study how cropping patterns change across regions
steps: Identify three regions of India with different cropping patterns
List the crops grown in each region
Explain how climate, soil and irrigation influence these patterns
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand regional diversity in agriculture

activities_projects.title: Agricultural Map Interpretation
steps: Mark the major rice, wheat and cotton producing states
Analyze the geographical reasons for their distribution
Write a short explanation of how physical factors influence these patterns
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve map interpretation and geographical analysis skills

activities_projects.title: Green Revolution Impact Assessment
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the effects of the Green Revolution
steps: Research the technologies introduced during the Green Revolution
Identify the regions where it had the greatest impact
Analyze both the positive and negative consequences of the revolution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about agricultural development

activities_projects.title: Agriculture and Industry Link Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Examine the connection between agriculture and industries
steps: Identify five industries dependent on agricultural raw materials
Explain how agricultural production affects these industries
Discuss how changes in agriculture may impact industrial growth
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand economic interdependence between sectors

activities_projects.title: Agricultural Reform Research
activities_projects.objective: Analyze institutional reforms in Indian agriculture
steps: Research reforms such as land reforms, crop insurance, and Kisan Credit Card
Explain the purpose of each reform
Evaluate their effectiveness in improving farmers' livelihoods
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain understanding of agricultural policy and governance

activities_projects.title: Plantation Agriculture Economic Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine the economic significance of plantation crops
steps: Choose two plantation crops such as tea and coffee
Analyze the climatic requirements and regions where they are grown
Explain their contribution to exports and employment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the economic role of plantation agriculture

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Agriculture Proposal
activities_projects.objective: Develop ideas for sustainable agricultural practices
steps: Identify three environmental problems related to agriculture
Suggest sustainable farming methods to address these issues
Explain how these methods can improve long-term productivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about sustainable development in agriculture

activities_projects.title: Future of Indian Agriculture Essay
activities_projects.objective: Encourage critical thinking about agricultural development
steps: Write an essay discussing current challenges in Indian agriculture
Include issues such as climate change, land fragmentation and irrigation
Suggest possible solutions for improving agricultural productivity
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical and writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of argument and clarity (10 marks)
